name: Forced registration
summary: The user is required to create an account before they can see prices, complete a purchase, read an article, or access content — even when the service could function anonymously or as a guest checkout.

evidence.claim: Brignull's dark-patterns taxonomy identifies 'forced enrollment' as a manipulative pattern where account creation is a gatekeeper to functionality that does not require it.
GDPR Article 6 requires a lawful basis for personal data collection; EDPB Guidelines 05/2020 prohibit conditioning service provision on consent for processing not necessary for the service.
FTC 2022 'Bringing Dark Patterns to Light' report includes registration-gating for price disclosure as a deceptive pricing practice under Section 5 FTC Act.

body.description: The site blocks access to information or functionality that does not require an account: pricing visible only after signup, guest-checkout hidden or removed, article content truncated behind a signup wall, or a download that requires email submission. The pattern conditions access on data collection that is not necessary for the interaction.
illegalInJurisdictions: EU-member-states (under GDPR Art 6, if consent is not freely given)

detectionHeuristics.description: Product listing shows 'Sign in to see price' or requires account for PDP access.
Checkout flow offers only 'Create Account' without 'Continue as Guest' option.
Content is truncated with a signup wall distinct from a paid subscription paywall — the gate is registration, not payment.
Whitepaper, PDF, or spec sheet download requires lead-gen form submission.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Inspect the page flow. Check: (1) can the user see pricing/specs/content without creating an account? (2) does checkout offer a guest option? (3) is the registration necessary for the stated service (e.g., saving orders), or gratuitous (seeing a price)? Flag verdict: necessary, convenience, or forced-registration.
body.remediation: Overlay Lens warning. If EU user, cite GDPR Article 6 (E2) and offer one-click path to DPA complaint. For US, recommend using a masked email service (e.g., Apple Hide My Email, Firefox Relay) to reduce identification friction. Highlight whether a guest-checkout option was hidden rather than absent.
